checks.notShotAlien: You didn't shoot down the alien.
checks.notShotAllAliens: You didn't shoot down all the aliens.

shootTheAliens.name: Track and shoot down all the aliens
shootTheAliens.description: Use getStartingAliensInRow() to get the alien positions, then move across the screen shooting them down. Track which aliens you've already shot by updating the arrays.

fireTheFireworks.name: Fire the fireworks when all aliens are shot down
fireTheFireworks.description: After shooting all the aliens, call fireFireworks() to celebrate. This must happen in the same loop iteration as shooting the final alien.

fireworksInsideLoop.name: Fire fireworks inside the loop
fireworksInsideLoop.description: Can you solve it by putting fireFireworks() within the repeat loop, rather than after it?

oneAlien.name: Shoot the alien
oneAlien.description: A single alien in the bottom row

oneRow.name: One sparse row
oneRow.description: Sparse aliens in the bottom row

threeRows.name: Three rows with fireworks
threeRows.description: Three rows of aliens — fire the fireworks when done!

fullRowsFireworks.name: Three packed rows with fireworks
fullRowsFireworks.description: A full grid — fire the fireworks when done!

fireworksInsideLoop.name: Fireworks inside the loop
fireworksInsideLoop.description: Can you fire the fireworks from within the repeat loop?

knowWhereAliens.question: How do I know where the aliens are if `isAlienAbove()` is gone?
knowWhereAliens.answer: Call `getStartingAliensInRow(1)`, `getStartingAliensInRow(2)`, and `getStartingAliensInRow(3)` once at the start. Each returns a list of 11 booleans you can store in variables and read from later.

whichAlienAbove.question: How do I work out which alien is above me right now?
whichAlienAbove.answer: Track your horizontal position in a variable, updating it each time you move. That position is the index into the row lists, so `row1[position]` tells you if there is an alien above you in row 1.

stopDoubleShoot.question: How do I stop shooting the same alien twice?
stopDoubleShoot.answer: After calling `shoot()`, set that index in the row to `false`. Next time round the loop the same position will report no alien there.

whichRowToShoot.question: Which row should I shoot from when there are aliens stacked above me?
whichRowToShoot.answer: Check the rows from bottom to top. Shoot the lowest live alien in your column and stop checking further rows that iteration.

whenToFireFireworks.question: How do I know when to call `fireFireworks()`?
whenToFireFireworks.answer: After shooting, scan all three row lists. If every entry is `false`, you have just downed the last alien, so call `fireFireworks()` in the same loop iteration. A small helper like `allAliensDead()` keeps the loop tidy.

moveLeft.description: Moves the laser cannon **one position to the left**. If you try to move off the left edge of the screen, you'll lose the game!

moveRight.description: Moves the laser cannon **one position to the right**. If you try to move off the right edge of the screen, you'll lose the game!

shoot.description: Shoots the laser upwards. **Only shoot when there's an alien above you**, or you'll lose the game! The laser cannon overheats easily, so you must move before shooting again.

getStartingAliensInRow.description: Returns a list of 11 booleans representing the alien positions in the given row at the **start** of the exercise. Row `1` is the bottom row, `2` is the middle, and `3` is the top.

fireFireworks.description: Fires celebratory fireworks! **Only call this when all aliens have been shot down**, or you'll get an error.
